item.title: Carefully Curated Crap, Vol. 846
item.description:

23
24
25
26 Man Tax: Brian Miskell
Never bear more than one trouble at a time. Some people bear three kinds -- all they have had, all they have now, and all they expect to have. -Edward Everett Hale So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, **Imgur Creator Spotlight is now in your hands!! Having trouble finding people I haven't already featured recently. Now I'm going to just do who YOU nominate. Tag me or DM me and I'll feature whoever you pick.**
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Chirpity died on me, imgur. About 20 minutes ago. Right beside me.
item.description: She started jumping around like she caught her foot in something and I grabbed her and said everything's okay, but she lay on her side and yowled a couple times and just breathed her last. I think it was a heart attack or a stroke or something. Now I'm just sitting here fucking sobbing,.

item.title: Sunday Buffoonery Dumpy Doo
item.description:
"I'll bet he could reach that sock that fell behind the dryer!"
So you're telling me they'll die off soon? Interesting...
Nowadays they call that "stalking"
This must be why I'm 45 but only feel about 30 or so, and very rarely like "the adult" in the room.
"Just suck it up."
Well they have to maintain their Standard of Living, right? We can't just expect them to... settle for less, right??
Maybe they have a high-yield savings account?
I wonder what they DID do for food? A pair of lions won't wait for 40 days to be over with. They'll get cranky.
I'll drive!
"So... come here often?"
"just act natural..."
I have more digital friends than real life friends
I can't remember how I survived back then
"Did you see it? I couldn't see it! What was it??"
"right, right, yeah I got it... now tell me it all again."
item.dc:creator: ddubya42

item.title: Saw this elsewhere and it’s stuck in my head
item.dc:creator: clemdarkstarzero

item.title: With vibe code, comes great vulnerabilities
item.description:
I still code projects by hand. I use AI to supplement my tasks, but I’m deliberate with what it does. I coded software to mitigate vulnerabilities for like 15 years and I’ll be damned if I let a juiced autocorrect “decide” what my code does. I won’t be the one adding tech debt to the stack.
I think this tag was vibe coded
item.dc:creator: LeftRightThere

item.title: Abandon Them
item.description: A general strike has to mean not going to work, not shopping online or in person, not going to restaurants or bars, not paying admission fees to anything, and not engaging in any form of commerce with anyone. Shut the whole economy down. Three days would be ideal, but even if it's just one day, it is time for a general strike.
item.dc:creator: Resistance1000

item.title: Tomorrow is my 9th Anniversary at therapy
item.description: Its hard for me to believe tomorrow will be my 9th anniversary at therapy. Im really proud of my progress. Im getting closer to my goals. It has definitely been a tough year but im still working hard. I hope everyone has a wonderful day. Thank you for all the encouragement and support it really appreciate everything.

item.title: Memes vol 684
item.description:
The funniest thing about this image is the random soccer ball emoji
Me
“He just left” and “that’s actually hilarious” some of my most used quotes. guys my coworker told me that he’s never met anybody who’s seen as many movies as I have and who quotes them so often and I feel like I haven’t even seen that many… I do quote though. That is a thing I do
Conventionally attractive. Autocorrect tried changing conventionally into convenia- I can’t possibly talk about it that much
names Bonnie
https://youtu.be/VGIjFBFae3Y?si=zMv4uxmqSc5MijLa
I made my computer background at work my baby monkey so I don’t go completely insane. And I printed him out and my coworkers put a mustache on him.
I LOVE GRAY CATS
Future tony award winning Joshua Henry intermission
I LOVE GRAY CATS
Bruce tax
Gertie tax
Nessie tax
item.dc:creator: danat101

item.title: Boy was bullied for wearing homemade University of Tennessee t-shirt, so they made it their own official design.
item.description: In 2019, an elementary school student in Altamonte Springs, Florida wanted to take part in “College Colors Day” and support his favorite team, the University of Tennessee Volunteers. Since he didn’t own any official merch, he hand drew the “U.T.” logo on a piece of paper and pinned it to a plain orange shirt. His teacher, Laura Snyder, said he was excited to show it off that morning, but by lunchtime some classmates mocked the homemade design, leaving him heartbroken and in tears. Wanting to cheer him up, Snyder shared the story online hoping someone connected to the university might send him a small gift. Instead, the story went viral. Thousands of Tennessee fans rallied behind the student, and the university responded by sending him a huge box of official gear. Then they went even further. The University of Tennessee turned the boy’s exact hand drawn design into an official t shirt sold by the school, with proceeds supporting anti bullying efforts. Demand became so massive that the university’s online store reportedly crashed from the flood of orders. What started as a moment of bullying turned into a story celebrated across the country, with thousands of people proudly wearing the young fan’s design.
item.dc:creator: kenistod

item.title: GovBrief Today #472
item.description: Louisiana Gov. Jeff Landry landed in Greenland today for a conference he wasn't invited to. Trump named Landry his Greenland envoy after Denmark and Greenland both refused to entertain a U.S. takeover of the island. Not a single Greenlandic politician is scheduled to meet with him. Two other governments contradicted him on the record today. China called the Beijing summit deals "preliminary," walking back his claims on tariffs, agriculture, and the sale of 200 Boeing jets. Meanwhile, Taiwan bristled at Trump floating a previously agreed arms sale as leverage with Beijing. Trump announces wins even when there aren't any. After thousands of documented lies in office, Americans now have to wait for the other country to confirm what he says. Use 5 Calls or Resistbot to demand Congress investigate what these governments actually agreed to, before the White House announces another one. I've emailed 7,445 stories over 472 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Don't miss today's quote of the day, images, and data on the website: https://go.govbrief.today/260517-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/china-preliminary [2] https://go.govbrief.today/taiwan-arms [3] https://go.govbrief.today/landry-nuuk [4] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-may17 [5] https://go.govbrief.today/ebola-who [6] https://go.govbrief.today/navy-air [7] https://go.govbrief.today/kean-father [8] https://go.govbrief.today/rededication [9] https://go.govbrief.today/wh-helipad Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Kinda sucks ass
item.description: Just need to vent because I can't anywhere else. Worked long and hard on a project in my field, was a multiple year design. Submitted into a category for a nationally recognised award. The submission paper was heavily edited to recognise a "company effort", keywords changed to address a "team attitude". Hadn't heard back for some time so i followed up. Found the submission was shortlisted as a finalist and the boss has asked for a head of department to accept the award on behalf of the company. I was extended an invite to attend out of courtesy....I am just so frustrated and angry with this whole thing. All this work and I dont think i will be recognised in any capacity, doubtful even within my own company...anyway thanks reading...
item.dc:creator: Therepostingposterwithoutanytitleperson

item.title: Christian Hypocrisy
item.description: “Christian” nationalists have reduced Christian from a standalone noun to an adjective that they hide behind when it’s convenient, but otherwise ignore. Jesus is too woke for them, so they ignore him. “Feed the poor? Are you kidding me? Ha!”
item.dc:creator: Resistance1000

item.title: Dump on how things are done. Some nice things I learned here. Enjoy !
item.description:
Choose between the "Don't stick your..." or "Paige no...." caption.
Patching up a spot in the wall.
Pattern on concrete floor.
Nicely done and clean. But attention, it's not always the best treatment for the tiles.
Bathtub mould.
Pool cleaning robot.
Applying silicone sealant in the bathroom.
Cap embroidery machine.
Cricket Sorter.
Induction pipe bender.
Leather strap hole punch.
The Springelator.
Perfectly cut metal pieces.
Ring remover.
Rolling ink onto a linocut printing block.
Full restoration of an old pan.
Shrink wrap over-underer.
Wheel squisher.
A Crate stacker !
Two trains docking together Setting up an underwater breathing mask.
600 kph Maglev train’s cockpit in China.
Two trains docking together.
130 meter long rails being transported. They seem to be very bend, but it's an illusion due to heavy zooming.
Hand-painting porcelain patterns so perfectly. Wow wow wow. Now I want one.
Jaffa Cakes falling off a conveyor belt for their chocolate coating.
Soap cutter.
Sand-fixing agent for patching damaged driveway. Easy as that.
The art of optical fiber splicing, which requires extreme precision.
Weaving steel wire mesh.
A Veggie washer !
Dozens of fishermen end up losing body parts to wolf fish. This is because many people don't realize that even after being "dead", and without a body, it is still capable of this ...
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot
